body:
### Problem

When changing the buffer shown in a float window with `style = "minimal"`, the local value of a global-local option is lost. This is not the case in `0.11.6` and it may be a consequence of https://github.com/neovim/neovim/commit/5943a81fe79a0d9a639af88452050a5056e9027d .

### Steps to reproduce

1. `nvim repro.lua` where `repro.lua` contains the following

```lua
-- default (global?) value of `listchars` is printed
print(vim.wo.listchars)

local buf1 = vim.api.nvim_create_buf(true, true)
local buf2 = vim.api.nvim_create_buf(true, true)
local minimal_win = vim.api.nvim_open_win(buf1, true, {
	relative = "editor",
	row = 0,
	col = 0,
	width = 10,
	height = 10,
	style = "minimal",
})
vim.api.nvim_set_option_value("listchars", "extends:…,precedes:…", { scope = "local", win = minimal_win })
-- new value (local) of `listchars` is printed
print(vim.wo.listchars)

vim.api.nvim_win_set_buf(minimal_win, buf2)
-- default (global?) value of `listchars` is printed
print(vim.wo.listchars)

-- the previously set local value of `listcharts` should be printed, but the
-- default (global?) one is used instead
vim.api.nvim_win_set_buf(minimal_win, buf1)
print(vim.wo.listchars)
```

2. `:source`

### Expected behavior

The local value of global-local options (in this case `listchars`) should be remembered for floating windows with `style = "minimal"`. This is the behavior in `0.11.6`, but not in the nightly release

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

NVIM v0.12.0-dev-2462+g1a02896e16

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

N/A

### Operating system/version

Arch linux latest

### Terminal name/version

wezterm 20240203-110809-5046fc22

### $TERM environment variable

wezterm

### Installation

pacman

labels.description: wrong behavior that was introduced in a previous commit (please bisect)
labels.name: bug-regression

title: Local value of global-local options is not maintained for `style = "minimal"` floating windows when changing buffers
